Well, that's, oh yeah, I have my little ugly list and I'm pretty good at getting rid of, you know, minor irritants, but I mean the emotional state. You get rid of one thing that drives you nuts, and the next thing grows in importance. You're always going to be irritated about something. I suspect I seek it. Merry Christmas. I'm just saying that this makes me a little bit more angry. Principal of organization, this really starts to be the answer. The thing I get asked most of course is, how the hell can I do this faster? All right, because there's a lot of it to do and there's only so much time and, you know, all of that. So if you want to be prolific and you want to be fast, it's important to have an inventory of material from which you draw repetitively, often most of what you do from. As you get something new, you need to put it in the inventory so it's there when you need it. So one organizational tool is thinking in terms of story line. So you may use the same stories, but you may use them in a different order in order to make a particular point. You're not delivering your entire chronology every single time because obviously you'd be delivering a book when you needed to deliver a postcard or you needed to deliver a weekly fact. But there's always a story line, meaning there's a beginning, there's a middle, there's an end, and we'll talk about formulas in a second. And then to actually build your inventory of stories themselves. And when you start to catalog them, there's lots of different ways to catalog them. We'll talk about several. But one is viewing each story has an event and most story events fit one of these four categories. Most of the kinds of stories we'd be using fit one of these four categories. So one is the achievement of something through conflict with others or with self. So you have a whole category of stories that represent triumph over conflict. You have another set that are adversity stories. So those are the ones pretty much everybody's got and everybody's most familiar with. And they are often probably the most useful to us. There is achieved through discovery stories. So an example of that that you're all familiar with is Malz's plastic surgeon and I'm doing these operations and all these people should be feeling better about themselves because now they look so much better but they look in the mirror and they see the same thing they saw before. Therefore, I've discovered the inner image, the self image is more in control than the outer image. So he has a story of discovery. It's not, it didn't come about through any adversity, it didn't come about through any conflict, it really didn't come about through scientific process. It came about through observation of what was happening consistently in front of him. Through a teacher or a mentor. That's a category of story that is pretty useful to most of us because we're selling, mentoring, coaching, training, education. And so the ever popular two minute lunch story which I've used for every infomarketer entering a niche who wasn't from the niche. And so he's a great marketer having lunch with somebody from that niche begging him to come into the niche and do business there and he gives the guy five things to do and the guy comes back and they have lunch a month later and the guy's revolutionized his business and made a million dollars. And so the classic blackie story is the, it actually comes from the gambling industry but a blackie story is some wise old person usually on their death bed giving you, giving you their secret with their last dying breath and you're the one to carry it on into the world. Some variation of that. The Carnegie to Hill story. So those are all achieved through the influence of some other person. And those are really the four big categories that we deal with. Is that going to be different for each story you do? So you can, in your origination story, it might be different than the story about a particular round of the league? Yeah, I mean the ultimate result here is you want, if you want to think about it this way, you want a file cabinet so that when you need one for a particular purpose you can go there and the ones who fit the purpose are readily accessible in the file cabinet. This is how we fill the file cabinet. Then a third way to approach this is through the values.
